questions.question: A bacterial colony doubles in size every 3 hours. If the colony starts with 500 bacteria, how many bacteria will be present after 9 hours?
questions.explanation: The colony doubles 3 times in 9 hours (9 ÷ 3 = 3). Use the formula A = 500 · 2³ = 500 · 8 = 4000.

questions.question: A radioactive substance decays according to the formula N(t) = N₀(0.5)^(t/5), where t is in days. If N₀ = 80 grams, what is N(10)?
An investment grows according to A(t) = 2000(1.04)^t, where t is the number of years. By what factor does the investment grow each year?
questions.explanation: In exponential form A = P(1 + r)^t, the base (1 + r) represents the growth factor. Here the base is 1.04, meaning the investment multiplies by 1.04 annually.

questions.question: A car depreciates in value such that it loses 15% of its value each year. If the car is worth $20,000 today, what will it be worth after 2 years? (Round to the nearest dollar.)
questions.explanation: Losing 15% means retaining 85% each year. Use A = 20000(0.85)² = 20000 · 0.7225 = 14450 dollars.

questions.question: A population of insects triples every 4 weeks. If there are currently 120 insects, in how many weeks will there be at least 3240 insects?
questions.explanation: Set up 120 · 3^(t/4) = 3240. Divide: 3^(t/4) = 27 = 3³. So t/4 = 3, giving t = 12 weeks.

questions.question: Which function models exponential decay: f(x) = 5(1.2)^x or g(x) = 5(0.8)^x? Write the letter of the correct function.
questions.explanation: Exponential decay requires a base between 0 and 1. Since 0.8 < 1, g(x) decays; since 1.2 > 1, f(x) grows.
